title: Isolating DNA from Wolbachia and Drosophila with the Puregene Tissue Kit
author: Alphaxand Njogu
materials: Centrifuge tubes, 1.5 mL, safe-lock, PCR clean

steps.title: Transfer Centrifuge tubes, 1
steps.instruction: Transfer Centrifuge tubes, 1.5 mL, safe-lock, PCR clean Eppendorf Catalog #22363212 containing sample to a bead-mill homogenizer. Homogenize at 1500 rpm, Room temperature, for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Add 301
steps.instruction: Add 301.5 µL lysis mixture to each sample.

steps.title: Transfer samples to an ice block
steps.instruction: Transfer samples to an ice block. Incubate for 00:01:00 to quickly cool the samples.

steps.title: Add 100 µL protein precipitation solution to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL protein precipitation solution to each sample. Immediately vortex each sample for 00:00:20 . It is important that the samples are immediately mixed after the protein precipitation solution is added. Failing to do so can impact quality of the precipitation.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 00:03:00 at 16000 rpm,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 00:03:00 at 16000 rpm, Room temperature, to pellet the protein. If the protein pellet is not tight, incubate On ice for 00:05:00 and repeat the centrifugation.

steps.title: Create lysis mixture containing the following,...
steps.instruction: Create lysis mixture containing the following, per sample in a Centrifuge tubes, 1.5 mL, safe-lock, PCR clean Eppendorf Catalog #22363212 : 300 µL cell lysis solution 1.5 µL Proteinase K Prepare a bit extra to account for pipetting errors.

steps.title: Mix by inverting the tubes 25 times
steps.instruction: Mix by inverting the tubes 25 times. To process large batches, arrange tubes in a tube rack. Place a second rack on top, sandwiching the tubes. Invert the entire stack to simultaneously invert all tubes.

steps.title: Add 300 µL Isopropanol, molecular biology...
steps.instruction: Add 300 µL Isopropanol, molecular biology grade Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP-2618-1 to a clean Centrifuge tubes, 1.5 mL, safe-lock, PCR clean Eppendorf Catalog #22363212 . Carefully transfer the supernatant from the prior step to the isopropanol by pipetting.

steps.title: Add 300 µL of 70 %...
steps.instruction: Add 300 µL of 70 % (v/v) ethanol to each sample.

steps.title: Add between 15 µL and 100...
steps.instruction: Add between 15 µL and 100 µL DNA hydration solution to each sample. The choice of elution volume depends on the starting material. For low biomass samples, a lower volume is preferred.

steps.title: Incubate at 55 °C for between...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 55 °C for between 03:00:00 and 16:00:00 in a dry-bath incubator. (Optional) If using a Thermomixer, set to shake at 300 rpm, °C, .

steps.title: (Optional) Dilute 20 µg/µL Glycogen, molecular...
steps.instruction: (Optional) Dilute 20 µg/µL Glycogen, molecular biology grade Invitrogen Catalog #10814-010 to 0.5 µg/µL . Add 1 µL to each sample. Glycogen acts as a carrier molecule, improving DNA precipitation yield, particularly in low-biomass samples.

steps.title: Mix by inverting the tubes 50 times
steps.instruction: Mix by inverting the tubes 50 times.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 16000 rcf,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 16000 rcf, Room temperature, to pellet the DNA.

steps.title: Carefully discard the supernatant by pouring into a waste container
steps.instruction: Carefully discard the supernatant by pouring into a waste container. Drain the tube by inverting on a paper towel. Take care that the pellet remains in the tube. A single, rapid inversion is essential. Allowing the liquid to settle back to the bottom can dislodge the DNA pellet, leading to loss of yield.

steps.title: Mix by inverting the tubes 50 times
steps.instruction: Mix by inverting the tubes 50 times.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 16000 rcf,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 16000 rcf, Room temperature, to pellet the DNA.

steps.title: Washing the pellet once more
steps.instruction: Washing the pellet once more: go to step #14 .

steps.title: Carefully discard the supernatant by pouring into a waste container
steps.instruction: Carefully discard the supernatant by pouring into a waste container.

steps.title: Transfer samples to a vacuum centrifuge
steps.instruction: Transfer samples to a vacuum centrifuge. Dry the DNA pellets for 00:10:00 . If using a Vacufuge plus, use the D-AL setting. Overdrying the DNA pellet can significantly reduce its solubility. It is important to stop the drying process before the pellet becomes completely dry.

steps.title: Vortex for 00:00:05 to break up the DNA pellet
steps.instruction: Vortex for 00:00:05 to break up the DNA pellet.

steps.title: Choose 1
steps.instruction: Choose 1: Incubate at 65 °C for 01:00:00 to dissolve the DNA. Incubate at Room temperature Overnight to dissolve the DNA.

steps.title: Use immediately or store at
steps.instruction: Use immediately or store at: -80 °C for long-term storage -20 °C for use within a few weeks 4 °C for use within a few days
